title: Honours for L. Cassius Manilianus

editionAuthor.title: Remarques sur Lucius Cassius Manilianus et sa Carriere
editionAuthor.author: Jacques
editionAuthor.citation: F. Jacques, « Remarques sur Lucius Cassius Manilianus et sa Carriere », Zeitschrift für Papyrologie und Epigraphik 84 (1990): 163‑70

support: A stele of the local compact white limestone, broken in three pieces, with an area of loss/damage in the centre, and a small section missing from the middle right margin.

layout.p: The text is laid out in 11 lines over the upper part of the stone, filling the available width, with the last line centred and a vacat below. The initial lines are slightly more widely spaced.

handNote.lettering: Tall narrow letters, but the first three lines show a wider module and straighter, more 'monumental' style.

provenanceFound._: Found in the area of Piazza Mokarta. When first presented in editions in the 1980s the three fragments were joined together and set in a wall; in 2002 they were separately in the courtyard of the biblioteca communale; in 2015 at least the main fragment was on display in the Museo Mirabilia Urbis.

provenanceObserved._: No autopsy, but observed by Prag 2002-07-01 and Metcalfe 2015-11-12

museum.name: Mirabilia Urbis
museum.description: A selection of architectural pieces of various dates is scattered on the floor around the outer parts of the church, with no labels or information of any kind. The centre of the church is given over to 6 display cases containing finds from several contrada around Mazara

location.address: Chiesa di San Bartolomeo, Piazza San Bartolomeo, 91026 Mazara del Vallo (TP)
